methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Orbital Composites is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Cole Nielsen that has raised $6.6M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a broader robotics and manufacturing peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable, though direct peer multiples at the seed stage are limited.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised as a proxy base value of $2M, application of a 2.5x multiplier—typical for progression from seed to the next funding round—yields an implied valuation of $6M. A peer-median approach could not be reliably applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow robotics and manufacturing sector; while 12 same-sector peers exist broadly, fewer than three qualify as true methodological matches, making sectoral aggregation unreliable.

The triangulated valuation range is $5M to $7M, with a median of $6M supported by the stage-progression framework. This range reflects the single applicable methodology and inherent uncertainty in seed-stage valuations where comparable transaction data remains sparse. Key uncertainties include the timing and competitive intensity of the next funding round, which would substantively influence the realized step-up multiple, as well as product-market fit validation and team execution relative to peer cohorts.
